questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $h(x)=x^{x^x}$, then at $x=1, \frac{h^{\prime}(x)}{h(x)}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{aligned} h(x) & =x^{x^{x^*}} \\ \Rightarrow \quad \log h(x) & =x^x \log x\end{aligned}$
On differentiating w. r. t. $x$, we get
as $\begin{aligned} \frac{h^{\prime}(x)}{h(x)} & =x^x \cdot \frac{1}{x}+x^x(1+\log x) \log x \\ & =x^{x-1}+(1+\log x) \log h(x)[\text { from Eq. (i) }] \\ & =1+(1+\log 1) \log h(x) \\ & =1+\log h(x)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y=A \cos n x+B \sin n x$, then $y_2+n^2 y$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$y=A \cos n x+B \sin n x$
On differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get
$y_1=-A n \sin n x+B n \cos n x$
Again differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get
$\Rightarrow \quad \begin{aligned} & y_2=-A n^2 \cos n x-B n^2 \sin n x \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y_2=-n^2(A \cos n x+B \sin n x) \\ & y_2=-n^2 \cdot y \Rightarrow y_2+n^2 y=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $u=x y^2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)$, then $x \frac{\partial u}{\partial x}+y \frac{\partial u}{\partial y}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that,
$\begin{aligned} & u=x y^2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \\ & \frac{\partial u}{\partial x}=y^2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)+x y^2 \cdot \frac{1}{1+\frac{y^2}{x^2}} \cdot\left(-\frac{y}{x^2}\right) \\ &=y^2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-\frac{x y^3}{x^2+y^2} \\ & \frac{\partial u}{\partial y}=2 x y \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)+x y^2 \cdot \frac{1}{1+\frac{y^2}{x^2}} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) \\ &=2 x y \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)+\frac{x^2 y^2}{x^2+y^2} \\ & \therefore \quad x \frac{\partial u}{\partial x}+ y \frac{\partial u}{\partial y}=x y^2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-\frac{x^2 y^3}{x^2+y^2} \\ & x \frac{\partial u}{\partial x}+y \frac{\partial u}{\partial y}=3 x y^2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x}=3 u\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $f(x)=\sqrt{a x}+\frac{a^2}{\sqrt{a x}}$, then $f^{\prime}(a)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
f(x)=\sqrt{a x}+\frac{a^2}{\sqrt{a x}}
$$
On differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f^{\prime}(x)=\sqrt{a} \cdot \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{x}}+\frac{a^2}{\sqrt{a}} \cdot\left(-\frac{1}{2 x \sqrt{x}}\right) \\
& f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{\sqrt{a}}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}-\frac{a \sqrt{a}}{2 x \sqrt{x}} \\
& f^{\prime}(a)=\frac{\sqrt{a}}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{a}}-\frac{a \sqrt{a}}{2 a \sqrt{a}}=\frac{1}{2}-\frac{1}{2}=0
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $u(x, y)=y \log x+x \log y$, then $u_x u_y-u_x \log x-u_y \log y+\log x \log y$ is equal to :
questions.solution:
We have,
$u(x, y)=y \log x+x \log y$
On differentiating partially w.r.t. $x$ and $y$ respectively,
$u_x=\frac{y}{x}+\log y, u_y=\log x+\frac{x}{y}$
Now, $u_x u_y-u_x \log x-u_y \log y+\log x \log y$
$=\left(\frac{y}{x}+\log y\right)\left(\log x+\frac{x}{y}\right)-\left(\frac{y}{x}+\log y\right) \log x$ $-\left(\log x+\frac{x}{y}\right) \log y+\log x \log y$
$=1+\frac{y}{x} \log x+\frac{x}{y} \log y+\log x \log y$
$-\frac{y}{x} \log x-\log x \log y-\log x \log y$
$-\frac{x}{y} \log y+\log x \log y$
$=1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\frac{d}{d x} \sin ^{-1}\left(3 x-4 x^3\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{d}{d x}\left\{\sin ^{-1}\left(3 x-4 x^3\right)\right\} \\ & \text { Put } x=\sin \theta \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \theta=\sin ^{-1} x \\ & =\frac{d}{d x}\left\{\sin ^{-1}\left(3 \sin \theta-4 \sin ^3 \theta\right)\right\} \\ & =\frac{d}{d x}\left\{\sin ^{-1} \sin 3 \theta\right\} \\ & =\frac{d}{d x}\{3 \theta\}=3 \frac{d}{d x} \sin ^{-1} x=\frac{3}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y=a \sin x+(5+2 x) \cos x$, then $y^{\prime \prime}+y=$
questions.solution:
$y=a \sin x+(5+2 x) \cos x$
Differential w.r.t ' $x$ '
$$
\begin{aligned}
& y^{\prime}=a \cos x+(5+2 x)(-\sin x)+(0+2) \cos x \\
& y^{\prime}=a \cos x-(5+2 x) \sin x+2 \cos x
\end{aligned}
$$
Again differential w.r.t ' $x$ '
$$
\begin{aligned}
& y^{\prime \prime}=-a \sin x-[(5+2 x) \cos x \\
& \quad+(0+2) \sin x]+2(-\sin x) \\
& \Rightarrow y^{\prime \prime}=-a \sin x-(5+2 x) \cos x-2 \sin x-2 \sin x \\
& \Rightarrow y^{\prime \prime}=-(a \sin x+(5+2 x) \cos x)-4 \sin x \\
& \Rightarrow y^{\prime \prime}=-y-4 \sin x \\
& \Rightarrow y^{\prime \prime}+y=-4 \sin x
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mtable><mtr><mtd><mi>x</mi></mtd><mtd><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mtd><mtd><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd><mn>1</mn></mtd><mtd><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mtd><mtd><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd><mn>0</mn></mtd><mtd><mn>2</mn></mtd><mtd><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi></mtd></mtr></mtable></mfenced></math>, then the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>''</mo></msup><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
if fx=fx=xx2x312x3x2026x

f'x=12x3x212x3x2026x+xx2x3026x026x+xx2x312x3x2006

=0+0+xx2x312x3x2006

=6x2

f''x=12x

f''(x):f'(x)

=12x:6x2

=2:x

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{x+5}{(x+1)^2(x+2)}\right)=$
questions.solution:
Let $\frac{x+5}{(x+1)^2(x+4)}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B}{(x+1)^2}+\frac{C}{(x+4)}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow x+5=A(x+1)(x+2)+B(x+2)+c(x+1)^2 \\
& \Rightarrow x+5=x^2(A+C)+x(3 A+B+2 C)+2 A+2 B+C .
\end{aligned}
$$
On comparing the coefficients of like power of $x$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
A+C & =0, \\
3 A+B+2 C & =1 \text { and } 2 A+2 B+C=5
\end{aligned}
$$
On substituting $A=-C$ in last two equations, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
B-C & =1 \\
2 B-C & =5
\end{aligned}
$$
and
$$
2 B-C=5
$$
On subtracting Eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get
$$
B=4 \Rightarrow C=3
$$
[from Eq. (ii)]
and hence $A=-3$
Now, $\frac{(x+5)}{(x+1)^2(x+2)}=\frac{-3}{(x+1)}+\frac{4}{(x+1)^2}+\frac{3}{(x+2)}$
On differentiating both sides w.r.t $x$, we get.
$$
\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{(x+5)}{(x+1)^2(x+4)}\right)=\frac{3}{(x+1)^2}-\frac{8}{(x+1)^3}-\frac{3}{(x+2)^2}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y=\left(\sin ^{-1} 2 x\right)^2+\left(\cos ^{-1} 2 x\right)^2$, then $\left(1-4 x^2\right) y_2-4 x y_1=$
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
y=\left(\sin ^{-1} 2 x\right)^2+\left(\cos ^{-1} 2 x\right)^2
$$
On differentiating both sides w.r.t. $x$, we get
$$
\frac{d y}{d x}=2 \sin ^{-1} 2 x \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-(2 x)^2}} \cdot 2+2 \cos ^{-1} 2 x
$$
$$
\left(\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-(2 x)^2}}\right) \cdot 2
$$

$$
\Rightarrow \sqrt{1-4 x^2} \frac{d y}{d x}=4\left(\sin ^{-1} 2 x-\cos ^{-1} 2 x\right)
$$
Again differentiating both sides w.r.t. $x$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \sqrt{1-4 x^2} y_2+\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{1-4 x^2}}(-8 x) y_1 \\
& =4\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-4 x^2}} \cdot 2+\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-4 x^2}} \cdot 2\right) \\
& =\sqrt{1-4 x^2} y_2-\frac{4 x}{\sqrt{1-4 x^2}} y_1=8 \cdot \frac{2}{\sqrt{1-4 x^2}} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \quad\left(1-4 x^2\right) y_2-4 x y_1=16
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y=2 \cos (2 \log x)+3 \sin (2 \log x)$, then $x^2 y^{\prime \prime}+x y^{\prime}+2 y=$
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& y=2 \cos (2 \log x)+3 \sin (2 \log x) \\
\therefore \quad & y^{\prime}=-2 \sin (2 \log x) \cdot \frac{2}{x}+3 \cos (2 \log x) \cdot \frac{2}{x} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & x y^{\prime}=2[-2 \sin (2 \log x)+3 \cos (2 \log x)] \\
\Rightarrow & x y^{\prime \prime}+y^{\prime \prime}=2\left[-2 \cos (2 \log x) \cdot \frac{2}{x}-3 \sin (2 \log x) \cdot \frac{2}{x}\right] \\
\Rightarrow & x^2 y^{\prime \prime}+x y^{\prime}=-4[2 \cos (2 \log x)+3 \sin (2 \log x)] \\
\Rightarrow & x^2 y^{\prime \prime}+x y^{\prime}=-4 y \\
\Rightarrow & x^2 y^{\prime \prime}+x y^{\prime}+2 y=-2 y
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $z=\frac{y}{x}\left[\sin \frac{x}{y}+\cos \left(1+\frac{y}{x}\right)\right]$, then $x \frac{\partial z}{\partial x}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
z=\frac{y}{x}\left[\sin \frac{x}{y}+\cos \left(1+\frac{y}{x}\right)\right]
$$
$$
\begin{array}{r}
\frac{\partial z}{\partial x}=\frac{y}{x}\left[\cos \frac{x}{y} \cdot \frac{1}{y}-\sin \left(1+\frac{y}{x}\right)\left(-\frac{y}{x^2}\right)\right] \\
+\left(-\frac{y}{x^2}\right)\left[\sin \frac{x}{y}+\cos \left(1+\frac{y}{x}\right)\right] \\
x \frac{\partial z}{\partial x}=y\left[\frac{1}{y} \cos \frac{x}{y}+\frac{y}{x^2} \sin \left(1+\frac{y}{x}\right)\right] \\
-\frac{y}{x}\left[\sin \frac{x}{y}+\cos \left(1+\frac{y}{x}\right)\right] \\
\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{\partial z}{\partial x}=\cos \frac{x}{y}+\frac{y^2}{x^2} \sin \left(1+\frac{y}{x}\right)-z
\end{array}
$$
Similarly,
$$
y \frac{\partial z}{\partial y}=-\cos \frac{x}{y}-\frac{y^2}{x^2} \sin \left(1+\frac{y}{x}\right)+z
$$
On adding Eqs. (i) and (ii)
$$
x \frac{\partial z}{\partial x}=-y \frac{\partial z}{\partial y}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y=\sqrt{x+\sqrt{y+\sqrt{x+\sqrt{y+\ldots \infty}}}}$, then $\frac{d y}{d x}=$
questions.solution:
We have
$$
\begin{aligned}
& y=\sqrt{x+\sqrt{y+\sqrt{x+\sqrt{y+\ldots \infty}}}} \\
& y=\sqrt{x+\sqrt{y+y}} \Rightarrow y^2=x+\sqrt{2 y} \\
\Rightarrow & y^2-x=\sqrt{2 y} \Rightarrow\left(y^2-x\right)^2=2 y \\
\Rightarrow & y^4-2 x y^2+x^2=2 y \\
\Rightarrow & y^4-2 x y^2-2 y+x^2=0
\end{aligned}
$$
On differentiating w.r.t. to $x$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 4 y^3 \frac{d y}{d x}-2 y^2-4 y x \frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{2 d y}{d x}+2 x=0 \\
& \frac{d y}{d x}\left(4 y^3-4 x y-2\right)=-2 x+2 y^2 \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y^2-x}{2 y^3-2 x y-1}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $y=\frac{\sqrt[3]{1+3 x} \sqrt[4]{1+4 x} \sqrt[5]{1+5 x}}{\sqrt[7]{1+7 x} \sqrt[8]{1+8 x}}$. Then $\frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}$ at $x=0$ is
questions.solution:
$y=\frac{\sqrt[3]{1+3 x} \sqrt[4]{1+4 x} \sqrt[5]{1+5 x}}{\sqrt[7]{1+7 x} \sqrt[8]{1+8 x}}$
$\therefore \quad \log y=\frac{1}{3} \log (1+3 x)+\frac{1}{4} \log (1+4 x)$
$\begin{aligned}+\frac{1}{5} \log (1+5 x) & -\frac{1}{7} \log (1+7 x) \\ & -\frac{1}{8} \log (1+8 x)\end{aligned}$
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. $x$, we get
$$
\begin{array}{r}
\frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}=\frac{1}{3} \cdot \frac{1}{1+3 x} \cdot 3+\frac{1}{4} \cdot \frac{1}{1+4 x} \cdot 4+\frac{1}{5} \cdot \frac{1}{1+5 x} \cdot 5 \\
-\frac{1}{7} \cdot \frac{1}{1+7 x} \cdot 7-\frac{1}{8} \cdot \frac{1}{1+8 x} \cdot 8
\end{array}
$$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}=\frac{1}{1+3 x}+\frac{1}{1+4 x}+\frac{1}{1+5 x}-\frac{1}{1+7 x}-\frac{1}{1+8 x} \\ & \therefore \quad \frac{1}{1} \cdot\left(\frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}\right)_{x=0}=\frac{1}{1+0}+\frac{1}{1+0}+\frac{1}{1+0}-\frac{1}{1+0}-\frac{1}{1+0} \\ & \ldots[\text { At } x=0, y=1]\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow\left(\frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}\right)_{x=0}=1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $a x^2+2 h x y+b y^2=3$, then $\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=$
options: $\frac{\left(a x y+h x^2+b y x\right)}{(a x+b y)^2}$
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
a x^2+2 h x y+b y^2=3
$$

On differentiating with respect to $x$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 a x+2 h x \frac{d y}{d x}+2 h y+2 b y \frac{d y}{d x}=0 \\
& \frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{(a x+h y)}{(b y+h x)}
\end{aligned}
$$

Again differentiating with respect to $x$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 a+2 h x \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}+2 h \frac{d y}{d x}+2 h \frac{d y}{d x}+2 b\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2 \\
&+2 b y \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=0
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}(h x+b y)=-\left(a+2 h \frac{d y}{d x}+b\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2\right) \\
& \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}(h x+b y)= \\
&-\left(a+2 h\left(\frac{-a x-h y}{h x+b y}\right)+b\left(\frac{-a x-h y}{h x+b y}\right)^2\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
$\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}(h x+b y)=-$
$$
\left[\frac{a(h x+b y)^2-2 h(a x+h y)(h x+b y)+b(a x+h y)^2}{(h x+b y)^2}\right]
$$
$$
\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{3\left(h^2-a b\right)}{(h x+b y)^3}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y^{\cos x}=x^{\sin y}$, then $\frac{d y}{d x}=$
options: $\frac{y(x \sin x \log y+\sin y)}{x(\cos x-y \log x \cos y)}$
$\frac{y(x \sin x \log x-\sin y)}{x(\cos x+y \log x \cos y)}$
questions.solution:
It is given that
$y^{\cos x}=x^{\sin y}$
On taking logrithm both sides, we get $\cos x \log y=\sin y \log x$ On differentiating both side w.r.t. $x$, we get $\frac{1}{y}(\cos x) \frac{d y}{d x}-(\sin x) \log y$
$=\frac{1}{x} \sin y+(\log x)(\cos y) \frac{d}{d x}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{y} \cos x-(\log x) \cos y\right)=\frac{1}{x} \sin y+(\sin x) \log y \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y(\sin y+x \sin x \log y)}{x(\cos x-y \log x \cos y)}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $x=a\left\{\cos \theta+\log \tan \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)\right\}$ and $y=a \sin \theta$, then $\frac{d y}{d x}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that,
$$
x=a\left(\cos \theta+\log \tan \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)\right) \text { and } y=a \sin \theta
$$
On differentiating w.r.t. $\theta$ respectively, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{d x}{d \theta} & =a\left(-\sin \theta+\frac{1}{\tan \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)} \cdot \sec ^2 \frac{\theta}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\right) \\
& =a\left(-\sin \theta+\frac{1}{\sin \theta}\right)=\frac{a \cos ^2 \theta}{\sin \theta}
\end{aligned}
$$
and $\frac{d y}{d \theta}=a \cos \theta$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \frac{d y}{d x} & =\frac{d y / d \theta}{d x / d \theta}=\frac{a \cos \theta}{a \cos ^2 \theta / \sin \theta} \\
& =\tan \theta
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y^{2}=a x^{2}+b x+c, \quad$ where $\quad a, b, c$ are constants, then $y^{3} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $y^{2}=a x^{2}+b x+c$
On differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get $2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=2 a x+b$
Again differentiating w.r.t. $x$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}+2 y \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=2 a \\
\Rightarrow & y \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=a-\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\Rightarrow \quad y \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=a-\left(\frac{2 a x+b}{2 y}\right)^{2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad y \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{4 a y^{2}-(2 a x+b)^{2}}{4 y^{2}}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 4 y^{3} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=4 a\left(a x^{2}+b x+c\right)$
$-\left(4 a^{2} x^{2}+4 a b x+b^{2}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \quad 4 y^{3} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=4 a c-b^{2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad y^{3} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{4 a c-b^{2}}{4}=$ constant
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $x=\frac{1-t^{2}}{1+t^{2}}$ and $y=\frac{2 a t}{1+t^{2}}$, then $\frac{d y}{d x}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $x=\frac{1-t^{2}}{1+t^{2}}$ and $y=\frac{2 a t}{1+t^{2}}$
On differentiating w.r.t. $t$, respectively, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{d x}{d t} &=\frac{\left(1+t^{2}\right)(0-2 t)-\left(1-t^{2}\right)(0+2 t)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \\
&=\frac{-4 t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}
\end{aligned}
$$
and $\frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{\left(1+t^{2}\right) 2 a-2 a t(2 t)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}=\frac{2 a\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}$
$\therefore \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d t}{d x / d t}=\frac{a\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{-2 t}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{a\left(t^{2}-1\right)}{2 t}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $y=x^{n} \log x+x(\log x)^{n}$, then $\frac{d y}{d x}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $y=x^{n} \log x+x(\log x)^{n}$
$$
\begin{array}{c}
\frac{d y}{d x}=n x^{n-1} \log x+x^{n} \cdot \frac{1}{x}+x n(\log x)^{n-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) \\
+1 \cdot(\log x)^{n} \\
=x^{n-1}(1+n \log x)+(\log x)^{n-1}[n+\log x]
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The valuc of $f(4)-f(3)$ is
questions.solution:
$f(4)-f(3)=\Delta f(3)$
$=\Delta[f(2)+\Delta f(2)]$
$[\because \Delta f(2)=f(3)-f(2)]$
$=\Delta f(2)+\Delta^{2} f(2)$
$=\Delta f(2)+\Delta^{2}[f(1)+\Delta f(1)]$
$=\Delta f(2)+\Delta^{2} f(1)+\Delta^{3} f(1)$

questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\mathrm{y}=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{x-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{x+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right]$, then $\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=$
questions.solution:
Given $y=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{x-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{x+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right]$
Put $x=\cos \theta \Rightarrow \theta=\cos ^{-1} x$
$y=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\cos \theta-\sin \theta}{\cos \theta+\sin \theta}\right]=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{1-\tan \theta}{1+\tan \theta}\right]$
$\quad=\tan ^{-1}\left[\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\theta\right)\right]=\frac{\pi}{4}-\theta$
$\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\pi}{4}-\cos ^{-1} x$
$$
\begin{aligned} y &=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \\ y &=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Let <math><mi mathvariant="normal">ϕ</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo></math> be the inverse of the function <math><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi>f</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>′</mo></mrow></msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></math>, then <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mi>d</mi> <mrow> <mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow> </mfrac> <mi>ϕ</mi><mfenced> <mi>x</mi> </mfenced></mrow> </math> is equal to
questions.solution:
We have, ϕx=f-1(x)

⇒x=f[ϕx]

On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get

1=f'ϕx.ϕ'(x)

⇒ϕ'x=1f'ϕx ...(i)

Since, f′x=11+x5  (given)

f'ϕx=11+ϕx5

From Equation (i),

ϕ'x=1f'[ϕx]=1+ϕx5

questions.q: If <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then the absolute value of 2<math><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
y=tan-1secx-tanx
y=tan-11-sinxcosx
⇒y=tan-1cosx2-sinx22cos2x2-sin2x2
⇒y=tan-1cosx2-sinx2cosx2+sinx2
⇒y=tan-11-tanx21+tanx2
⇒y=tan-1tanπ4-x2
⇒y=π4-x2
Differentiating with respect to x, we get
⇒dydx=-12=-0.5

questions.q: Let <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>…</mo> <mi>∞</mi> </mrow></msup></mrow></msup></math>, then <math><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi></mrow></mfrac><mi> </mi></math>is equal to (given <math> <mrow> <mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></mrow> </math> )
questions.solution:
Given, y= x x x...∞ then y= x y

Taking ln  on both the sides, we get

lny=ylnx

⇒ 1y.dydx=y.1x+dydx.lnx

⇒dydx1y-ln⁡x=yx

⇒ dydx=y2x(1-ylnx)

questions.q: If <math><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> and <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi> </mi><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
∵x=1-t21+t2 and y=2t1+t2

Put t=tan⁡θ in both the equations, we get

x=1-tan2⁡θ1+tan2⁡θ=cos⁡2θ ...(i)

and y=2tan⁡θ1+tan2⁡θ=sin⁡2θ ...(ii)

On differentiating both the eqs.(i) and (ii), we get

dxdθ=-2sin⁡2θ and dydθ=2cos⁡2θ

Therefore, dydx=dydθdxdθ=-cos⁡2θsin⁡2θ=-xy

questions.q: If   (cos x) y = (siny) x , find  dy dx .
questions.solution:
We have,

   (cosx) y = (siny) x

Taking logarithms on both sides, we get

  y log cosx=xlogsiny

Differentiate with respect to x we get

  y log cosx=xlogsiny

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>)</mo></math> has a derivative at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow></munder><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
limx→axfa-afxx-a

Add and subtract af(a) 

=limx→axfa-afa+afa-afxx-a

=limx→afax-a-afx-fax-a

Separate and solve

=limx→afax-ax-a-limx→aafx-fax-a

As f'(a)=limx→af(x)-f(a)x-a (derivative of f(x) at x=a exist)

=fa-af′a

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,

y=tan-1sinx+cosxcosx-sinx

⇒y=tan-11+tanx1-tanx

⇒y=tan-1tanπ4+x

⇒y=π4+x

Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get

dydx=1
